author: y4rr
content:
Plan: Sprint V.1 — ConvNeXt (#930) + EfficientNet (#931) logit parity vs torchvision reference.

Approach:
1. Create scripts/regenerate_vision_v_fixtures.py — generates ferrotorch-vision/tests/conformance/fixtures_v_parity.json with torchvision seeded forward outputs (torch.manual_seed(42), input=randn(1,3,224,224), weights=None, model.eval()).
2. Extend conformance_vision_models.rs with full parity test entries for convnext_tiny and efficientnet_b0 — reads fixtures_v_parity.json, seeds ferrotorch weights identically, runs forward, asserts L2-diff vs reference within F32_MATMUL=1e-3.
3. If logit divergence is found, add per-stage intermediate assertions to localize; fix root cause in convnext.rs or efficientnet.rs.
4. Run cargo test -p ferrotorch-vision --test conformance_vision_models to verify clean.
5. Run cargo build --workspace + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets for workspace clean.
6. Close issues #930 and #931 via crosslink.

Key constraint: these are synthetic seeded weights (no download) so parity means same random-init formula produces same numerical outputs.
